1adn2pv.script:
YOU: {{Name}}! {{Agent}} here — we met at the open house on {{Address}} this afternoon. You were the one who loved the {{Feature}} — I remember because you went back to look at it twice.

So, real talk: what did you think once you got in the car?

(listen — then match their energy:)
If they loved it: "Here's what I know — two other groups asked for disclosures today. If this is the one, let's not let the weekend decide for you. I can have us in front of the sellers by tomorrow."
If lukewarm: "Totally fair. Tell me what was missing — because if it exists in {{Neighborhood}}, I'll find it. That's literally my job. What are the three non-negotiables?"

Either way: follow up, follow through, follow back. You'll hear from me Tuesday with what happened to the house — deal?

1p8bu0e.script:
YOU: {{Owner}}, {{Agent}}. I'll be direct because your time is expensive: I want to sell {{Address}}, and I want to tell you the one thing most agents won't.

Your home isn't going to sell because of its square footage. Nobody buys 6,000 feet — they buy the morning they wake up to that view, the dinner party in that kitchen, the life your home lets them tell people they have. That's what I sell.

Here's what that looks like in practice: cinematic film, not photos. A launch, not a listing. The right eyeballs in {{City}} AND out of state — because your buyer probably doesn't live here yet.

I'd like 45 minutes to show you the full playbook, including exactly what I'd spend on your launch and where. When works — Thursday, or the weekend?

kufwvr.script: {{Name}}! {{Agent}} — you walked through {{Address}} today and I could tell it landed different for you. Quick update you'll want: two groups requested disclosures tonight. If that house is IT, tomorrow morning matters. If it's not, tell me what was missing and I'll hunt down the version that has it — that's literally what I do. Either way: {{Phone}}, tonight or first thing. Follow up, follow through — that's the whole job.

mnjnzp.script: {{Name}} — {{Agent}} from yesterday's open house at {{Address}}. Promised update: offers are being called for {{Day}}. If you want in, I need 10 minutes today. If it wasn't the one — what was the miss? I'll fix it in the next search.

1o3jog3.script:
Subject: {{Address}} — where it stands (and where you stand)

{{Name}},

Great meeting you Saturday. Straight update, because momentum matters more than polish:

- {{N}} groups through this weekend
- {{M}} disclosure requests
- Sellers reviewing offers {{Day}}

If this is your house: call me today and let's build the offer that wins — price is only a third of it; terms and timing are the rest.

If it's not: even better. You've now told me exactly what "the one" needs that this one lacked. Give me a week and first refusal on what I find.

The market rewards decisiveness. So do I.

{{Agent}} · {{Brokerage}} · {{Phone}}

1tesw1i.script: {{Name}} — {{Agent}}. You asked about {{Address}} 4 minutes ago; here's the real story: {{Status}}. Two more like it (one unlisted) if this one's not perfect. Want both addresses? Speed wins in this market — that goes for agents too. 😄

g7x8cd.script: {{Owner}}, {{Agent}}. Your buyer probably doesn't live in {{City}} — that's the single most important fact about selling a home like yours. My last {{N}} closings at this level: buyers from out of state or overseas, found through film-quality marketing that travels, not yard signs that don't. I put together a two-minute concept of how {{Address}} launches to that audience. It'll be the best two minutes of marketing you've seen this year — {{Phone}}.

9agjzq.script: {{Owner}} — {{Agent}}, {{Brokerage}}. A serious buyer has asked me about homes in your enclave (nothing public, no listings involved). If a quiet, on-your-terms conversation about {{Address}} ever made sense, this is the low-pressure version of it. Worth a coffee?

17du4vu.script:
Subject: How {{Address}} should meet the world

{{Owner}},

Most listings are announcements. At your level, a listing should be a premiere.

Here's the launch I'd build for {{Address}}:
- THE FILM — 90 seconds, cinematic, the life not the layout. This is what travels to LA, New York, overseas.
- THE STORY — every great home has one; buyers pay for provenance.
- THE ROLLOUT — private preview for my buyer network first, then a coordinated public moment with momentum already built.
- THE FOLLOW-THROUGH — every inquiry answered within the hour, every showing followed up same-day. Deals die in the gaps; I don't leave gaps.

I'd love 45 minutes to present the full concept — spend included, timeline included. You'll leave knowing exactly what selling looks like, whether it's this year or someday.

{{Agent}} · {{Brokerage}} · {{Phone}}

lfi79n.script:
(coaching your buyer before they write in a competitive situation)

YOU: {{Name}}, real talk before we write this: there are {{N}} offers, and the winner won't just be the biggest number. Sellers pick certainty and story. So here's our three-part play:

ONE — THE NUMBER: strong enough to survive round one. I'll show you the comps for where that line is.

TWO — THE CERTAINTY: tightened timelines, deposit that signals commitment, only the contingencies you actually need. Every contingency you keep is a discount the seller mentally applies.

THREE — THE STORY: sellers are humans leaving a home they love. One paragraph about who you are and the life you'll live there — it's won me deals against higher offers, repeatedly.

You bring the number you're comfortable with. I'll bring two and three. Ready to build it?

36gfo3.script: {{Name}}, {{Agent}}. The deal hit a bump — the {{Issue}} — and I want you to hear my voice, not read a text, when I tell you: this is fixable, and bumps are where good agents earn their keep. I've already got two paths sketched out, and both keep you moving toward keys. Call me tonight, even late — we'll pick one and execute tomorrow morning. Deals don't die from problems; they die from silence. We don't do silence. {{Phone}}.

3l50mb.script: {{Name}} — update: seller reviewed our response, we're {{Gap}} apart, which in this market is NOTHING. Don't lose a house over {{PerMonth}}/month. I have one move left that usually closes gaps like this — 5-min call tonight?

188xtpg.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}}, local expert here in {{Neighborhood}}, and I'm calling about your property that just came off the market. I know, I know, you're probably thinking, 'Another agent, another call,' but bear with me for one minute. People don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends, and I want to be that friend who helps you get the outcome you want. Would you be open to me asking you one question about what happened with the listing? When you reflect on the experience, what do you think was the main reason it didn't sell? It sounds like... (listen and label their response). Last question: if you could sell your home without the hassle you just went through, would a 15-minute conversation about how to make that happen be worth your time? Let's keep more balls in the air and focus on the improved life you'll have once this is sold.
188xtpg.why: I rewrote the script to embody Ryan Serhant's voice, incorporating his philosophy and signature phrases, such as 'people don't like being sold' and 'keep more balls in the air', to create a more conversational and less sales-y tone.

eo1uc9.script: Hi {{Owner}}, this is {{Agent}} with {{Brokerage}}, and I'm calling about your home at {{Address}} that just came off the market. Did you sell it, or did the listing expire? (pause) Ah, I see. Well, let me tell you something - people don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends. I've RESEARCHED what makes a home sell, and I've got a proven track record of success. My team and I, we're not just about selling homes, we're about creating an improved life for our clients. If your home expired, it's not a reflection of the property itself, but rather how it was positioned in the market. The buyers who walked through your door weren't rejecting your home, they were rejecting the way it was presented. That's a fixable problem. I've got a 12-point plan that I use specifically for homes like yours, and it covers everything from pricing strategy to presentation and marketing. It's all about keeping more balls in the air, and not just focusing on one deal. I'd love to walk you through it, and show you how we can work together to get your home sold. When would be the best time for me to share it with you? How about today at 4, or tomorrow morning? Let's get your home sold, and let's get you the life you want.

1w6mndl.script: Hi, I'm calling about the home for sale — is this the owner? ... Great. {{Owner}}, my name's {{Agent}}, I'm a local agent with SERHANT. - and before you hang up, let me tell you, I'm not calling to sell you on giving me the listing, I'm calling because I believe people don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends. I want to be that friend to you in this process. Would it be ridiculous if I asked you just two questions? One: if you sell it yourself and it goes perfectly — where's the dream? Where are you moving to? (listen; people talk about the life, not just the house) Two: most owners I talk to sell on their own to protect their equity — is that fair for you too? (yes) Totally get it. I'm all about the three F's: follow-up, follow-through, and follow-back. So here's my only ask: if at some point the calls slow down and the showings turn into strangers wandering through... would you be opposed to having a backup plan already in place? No cost to have one. I'll check in in two weeks. If it's sold by then, I'll be the first to congratulate you. Remember, as someone who's all about Big Money Energy, I believe in keeping more balls in the air - let's keep the options open and see what works best for you.

1gwb9m9.script:
(call within 5 minutes of the inquiry — the odds of connecting drop 10x after that)

   YOU: Hey {{Name}}, it's {{Agent}}. I saw you were just checking out {{Address}} online, so I'm following up while it's top of mind. We've got 90 seconds, can you spare it?

   Let's keep more balls in the air and explore what's really driving your search. Three quick questions to get us started:
   One — is {{Address}} the exact kind of home you're looking for, or are we just getting started on the journey?
   Two — what's your timeline looking like: are we talking months or years?
   Three — have you had a chance to get pre-approved yet, or is that on your to-do list?

   (then deliver ONE piece of value:) Based on what you're looking for, here's the plan: I'll send you the top three matches in {{Neighborhood}} tonight — including one that's not even on the market yet. My goal is to be your go-to expert, your guide, and your partner in this process. If I'm not adding value, then I'm not doing my job. So, let's keep the conversation going and see if we can make some magic happen. Fair?

1ugh7xn.script:
{{Name}}? It's {{Agent}} — the one whose social and video DMs you've been crushing on for weeks, right? (laugh) I mean, let's be real, people don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends, and that's exactly what we're doing here.

      I'm calling because you engaged with my content on {{Topic}}, and honestly, the comment section wasn't the place to give you the full download. I've got a ton of value to share, and I want to make sure you get it. 

      Here's the thing: when it comes to {{Topic}}, the numbers are telling a story. (give the actual insight, with numbers — one full minute of pure value, nothing asked). I'm talking research-backed, data-driven insights that can change the game for you. It's all about keeping more balls in the air, and I'm not just talking about juggling multiple deals. I'm talking about creating an improved life, where every aspect is working together in harmony.

      Now, I know what you're thinking. You're thinking, 'How does this apply to me?' Well, let me tell you. As someone who's been in this industry for years, I've seen it all. And I've learned that it's not just about the transaction; it's about the relationship. It's about being a Finder, a Keeper, and a Doer — all at the same time. It's about following up, following through, and following back. And it's about understanding the Three F's: Follow-up, Follow-through, and Follow-back.

      So, is any of this research for a real move, or are you just a market nerd like me? Either answer's a good answer. I'm here to help, and I want to make sure you get the most out of our conversation.

1l1cj47.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}} from SERHANT. in {{Neighborhood}}. I'm calling because I noticed you own {{Address}}, but you're not local. Let me tell you, I work with a ton of absentee and investor owners, and I'm all about keeping it real. So, here's the deal. When it comes to your property, I know you want to shop with friends, not be sold to. You want to know the numbers, and you want to know them now. So, I've got three key things to share with you. First, what's the current rental potential of your property? Are you leaving money on the table? Second, what's the as-is sale value of your property today? And third, what could you do with that equity if you were to reinvest it in something with better returns and less stress? I can run all three scenarios for you in under an hour, at no cost to you. It's all about giving you the knowledge to make informed decisions. You'll be able to see exactly where you stand, and who knows, you might just find a better way to grow your wealth. So, what's your email? I'll send over the info, and we can go from there.

29vur3.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}}, your go-to expert in pre-foreclosure and NOD situations. I know you've been getting slammed with calls about {{Address}}, but let me tell you, most of those guys are just trying to make a quick buck. I'm not here to lowball you or take advantage of your situation. As someone who's RESEARCHED the market and understands the intricacies of pre-foreclosure, I can tell you that you've got more options now than you will in 60 days. Every week that passes, one of those options disappears. Let's get real, you might be able to reinstate and stay, modify the loan, sell with your equity protected - you might have more than you think, or even a short sale that saves your credit. I don't charge you a dime to lay out all your options, one conversation, one page, and you get to decide. I'm all about Follow-up, Follow-through, and Follow-back, so you can trust I'll be with you every step of the way. My daily routine is all about being a Finder, Keeper, and Doer, ensuring I've got multiple balls in the air, so I'm not desperate, and I can give you the best advice. I'll help you prioritize your Why, focus on the Work, and make it happen. Even if you never call me again, can we map out your options this week?

35zze9.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}} from {{City}}. Let's talk about the property taxes on {{Address}}. I know people don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends, and that's exactly how I approach this. The county's got it listed as tax-defaulted, but here's the thing: you're still in the redemption window, and that's a game-changer. You can bring it current, set up a payment plan, or if the property's become a burden, we can explore selling it and paying those taxes at closing - keeping your hard-earned equity intact. I'm all about keeping more balls in the air, and I don't want you to miss this opportunity. As someone who's RESEARCHED the ins and outs of tax-defaulted properties, I can tell you that time is of the essence. The clock is ticking, and if we don't act, the county can auction it off in five years, wiping out any equity above what's owed. That's not a scenario I want you to face. So, let's follow-up on this immediately, and I'll make sure to follow-through and keep you updated every step of the way. As the CEO of SERHANT., I've seen it time and time again - all-Doer agents starve their pipeline, but I'm a firm believer in allocating time across the Finder, Keeper, and Doer roles. I'd love to map out your options, free of charge, and show you the numbers this week before another penalty date hits. Can I schedule a time to go over this with you?

hs70en.script: Hey {{Name}}, it's {{Agent}}. I'm really sorry to hear about your loss. I know this call is out of the blue, but I want to help. As someone who's worked with a lot of families in similar situations, I know how overwhelming it can be to deal with an inherited property. My goal is to take things off your plate, not just the house, but all the other stuff that comes with it - the cleanout, utilities, yard work, insurance, all of it. I'm all about keeping more balls in the air, so you don't have to. Can I ask, what's the one thing that's weighing on you the most right now? Let me take that one thing off your plate, no strings attached. I'll handle it for you, and when you're ready, we can discuss the property. We can go over the numbers for keeping it, renting it, or selling it, and I'll help you make an informed decision. My approach is all about the three F's - follow-up, follow-through, and follow-back. I'll be with you every step of the way, and I'll make sure you have all the information you need to make the best decision for you and your family.
hs70en.why: I rewrote the script to reflect Ryan Serhant's voice, incorporating his philosophy and signature phrases, such as 'keeping more balls in the air' and the 'three F's', to make the script sound more natural and authentic.

16uf3g4.script: Hey {{Name}}, it's {{Agent}}. So, I was introduced to you by {{Referral Source}}. Let me tell you, I'm all about keeping more balls in the air, and I specialize in handling real estate sales during divorce. Now, I know what you're thinking - people don't like being sold, they like to shop with friends. That's why my approach is different. I represent the HOUSE, not either spouse. Transparency is key - same info, same time, in writing, to both parties. No 10pm calls to one side and voicemails to the other. That's how I maintain neutrality, and that's why family-law attorneys refer me. It's all about the Three F's: Follow-up, Follow-through, and Follow-back. So, here's what I'd do first: provide a written valuation that both parties can trust, create a sale timeline that fits the case timeline, and develop a communication plan that your attorneys will sign off on. The house is often the biggest asset and the biggest fight, but my goal is to make it neither. I'm about finding a solution that works for everyone, and that's what I call Big Money Energy. Would it be helpful if I sent over my one-pager on the process to both attorneys this week? Let's work together to make this process as smooth as possible.
16uf3g4.why: I rewrote the script to reflect Ryan Serhant's voice, incorporating his signature phrases and philosophy, such as 'keeping more balls in the air' and the 'Three F's', while maintaining the original structure and intent.

1bagf.script:
{{Owner}}, let's get real for a second. I know this house is more than just a property to you - it's where you've made memories, raised your family, and built a life. Twenty-two showings and no offer can be tough to swallow, I get it. It's like the market is saying, 'Hey, your home isn't worth what you think it is.' But here's the thing: what if lowering the price isn't about admitting defeat, but about being strategic? 
   
   Think about it. Buyers are out there, but they're using our price to negotiate a better deal on the house down the street. We're the comp that's making every other home in the neighborhood look like a steal. I'm a big believer in keeping more balls in the air, and right now, we've only got one ball in the air - and it's not moving. 
   
   My philosophy is all about the three F's: Follow-up, Follow-through, and Follow-back. And in this case, we need to Follow-up with a new pricing strategy. We need to be the Finder, the Keeper, and the Doer all at once. We need to find new buyers, keep the ones we have engaged, and do the work to get this deal done. 
   
   So, what if we repositioned at {{Price}}? Not as a concession, but as a calculated move to get more eyes on the property? More buyers, more competition, more chances to get you the best deal possible. It's all about Big Money Energy, and I truly believe this is the way to get it. 
   
   You see, people don't like being sold - they like to shop with friends. And that's exactly what we're going to do. We're going to make this house so appealing, so desirable, that buyers will be fighting to make an offer. And when that happens, we'll be in the driver's seat, negotiating from a position of power. 
   
   So, what do you say? Are you ready to make a move, to keep more balls in the air, and to get this deal done?
1bagf.why: The script was rewritten to incorporate Ryan Serhant's voice, pacing, and philosophy, with a focus on his signature phrases and negotiation strategies.

1wh3hhq.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}} with {{Brokerage}}, the go-to expert for Commercial & Multifamily in {{Submarket}}. I've been researching ownership trends, and I noticed you've held {{Address}} for {{Years}} years - that's a big milestone, and it's natural to wonder what your building's worth in today's market. So, I'm going to give it to you straight, no pitch, no charge: based on current cap rates for your asset class in this submarket, your building would trade somewhere in the range of {{Range}}, and that's not a guess, that's based on today's comps. Now, I know what you're thinking - what are my options? Well, let me tell you, as someone who's worked with numerous owners in your position, I've seen three common paths: you can choose to do nothing, which is perfectly valid; you can refinance, pull out some equity, and keep the asset; or you can trade up via 1031 into something bigger or more passive before the exchange math changes. My goal here isn't to list your building today, but to be the broker who already knows your asset inside and out when you're ready to make a move. Can I send you the full valuation in writing, so you have all the facts? Let's keep more balls in the air and explore how we can improve your life through smart real estate decisions.
1wh3hhq.why: I rewrote the script to reflect Ryan Serhant's voice, incorporating his signature phrases and philosophy, such as 'keep more balls in the air' and focusing on the improved life, while maintaining the original structure and intent.

9w2wap.script: Hey {{Owner}}, it's {{Agent}} — your go-to expert for all things real estate in {{City}}, and I'm calling about {{Address}} because I know we can unlock its full potential. People don't like being sold; they like to shop with friends, and that's exactly what I'm here to do — guide you through the process like a friend. You've got two solid options to sell a house that needs some love, and I want to break them down for you. Route one: we fix it up, list it, and you get top dollar. I can give you the lowdown on which repairs will give you the biggest bang for your buck and which ones are just going to burn a hole in your pocket — most sellers waste around ten grand on the wrong stuff, and I'm here to make sure you don't fall into that trap. Route two: we sell it as-is, no fuss, no muss. You won't have to deal with any repairs, cleanouts, or strangers traipsing through your home for months on end. It's less money, but way less hassle, and you get to close on your own terms. Now, most agents are going to push route one because it means a bigger paycheck for them, but I'm about keeping it real and giving you the best options for your situation. I can run you both scenarios side by side, and we can decide what's best for you. It'll take me about a day to get those numbers to you. You in?
9w2wap.why: I rewrote the script to reflect Ryan Serhant's voice, philosophy, and pacing, focusing on a more conversational tone and emphasizing the importance of transparency and client-centric approach.

11nu5e5.script: Buenas tardes, ¿hablo con {{Owner}}? Le habla {{Agent}}, su amigo en el mundo de los bienes raíces aquí en {{Neighborhood}}. ¿Cómo está? Escuche, no quiero venderle nada, solo quiero que sepa que acabamos de cerrar un trato en su calle y eso cambia el valor de su propiedad. ¿Sabe cuánto vale su casa hoy? Ese número es su dinero, y quiero asegurarme de que tenga acceso a esa información. ¿Le gustaría que le prepare un análisis personalizado y gratuito del valor de su casa? Sin compromiso, solo información. (if they switch to English) Of course, let's keep the conversation going! As I was saying, a home just sold on your street and that impacts everyone's value. I'd love to share the real number with you, free of charge. Would you prefer that via email or text? (cierre) Perfecto. Ahora, si algún familiar o amigo está pensando en comprar o vender, estoy aquí para ayudar, en español o inglés, como se sientan más cómodos. Recuerde, mi objetivo es ser su guía en este proceso, no solo un agente.
